name: Lean SDD
description: Streamlined SDD for solo developers and fast iteration: no Discovery/Planning phase, direct Spec→Tasks→Impl→Validate pipeline. Includes lean constitution template and simplified spec structure.

files..aura/presets/lean-constitution-template:
# Constitution: {{PROJECT_NAME}} (Lean Mode)
Version: 1.0

## Preamble
This is a lean-mode constitution for solo development or fast iteration. Three principles over nine — pick the constraints that matter most.

## Articles

### Article 1 — Boundary-First
Every implementation task must declare which files it is allowed to modify before writing code.
_Rationale:_ Prevents scope creep in fast-moving solo development.

### Article 2 — Tests Before Merge
Every task must have at least one passing test covering its primary acceptance criterion before being marked complete.
_Rationale:_ The minimal safety net for lean development.

### Article 3 — Spec Is Truth
If code and spec.md conflict, the spec wins. Update the code, not the spec (unless spec is amended via /aura-spec-refine).
_Rationale:_ Prevents code drift from becoming the de facto spec.

## Amendment Process
Amend any article by running `/aura-spec-refine` and noting the change in Amendment History.

## Amendment History
| Version | Date | Change |
|---------|------|--------|
| 1.0 | today | Initial lean constitution |

## Constitutional Gates (checked by /aura-plan)
- Gate 1: Does this plan violate Article 1 (Boundary-First)?
- Gate 2: Does this plan violate Article 2 (Tests Before Merge)?
- Gate 3: Does this plan violate Article 3 (Spec Is Truth)?
files..aura/presets/lean-spec-template.md:
# Spec: {{FEATURE_NAME}}

## Status
`draft` | created: {{DATE}}

## Problem
<!-- One paragraph: what is broken or missing -->

## Scope
**In:** <!-- What this spec covers -->
**Out:** <!-- What this spec explicitly excludes -->

## Boundary
**Modules touched:** <!-- e.g., src/auth/, src/api/users.ts -->
**Contracts:** <!-- e.g., exports TokenService.validate() -->

## Acceptance Criteria

1. WHEN [trigger] THE SYSTEM SHALL [action]
2. IF [condition] THE SYSTEM SHALL [response]
3. WHILE [state] THE SYSTEM SHALL [maintain behavior]

<!-- Lean mode: 3–5 ACs max. If you have more, split into two specs. -->

## Notes
<!-- Open questions, assumptions, dependencies -->
files..aura/presets/lean-workflow-guide.md:
# Lean SDD Workflow

This preset configures the lean pipeline: **Spec → Tasks → Impl → Validate**
(No Discovery or Planning phases)

## Usage

```
aura-sdd workflow run lean-sdd --input feature="my-feature"
```

## When to use lean mode

- Solo development
- Iteration on existing well-understood code
- Prototyping with known architecture
- Features that touch only 1–2 modules

## When to switch back to full SDD

- Touching 3+ modules
- Unclear requirements
- Team collaboration needed
- Performance or security implications

## Lean mode checklist

- [ ] Spec has 3–5 ACs (not 10+)
- [ ] Boundary is a single module or 2 files
- [ ] Tests exist for each AC
- [ ] `/aura-sync` passes (IN_SYNC) before merge
